seo.title: Butter - the great temptation - Marinda Kook

html:
Making butter is as simple as putting a whisk into cream. Nothing, and I mean nothing, comes close to homemade bread and butter. Everyone should make their own butter once in their life. It's something for your bucket list.

500 ml good quality cream – make sure the fat content is high

NOW:

  • let the cream stand outside the fridge until it's at room temperature – preferably
  • now start whisking slowly, either with a hand whisk or with your electric mixer
  • first it becomes like stiffly whipped cream – just keep whisking
  • then it becomes a bit crumbly – just keep whisking
  • now it definitely starts separating – just keep whisking
  • then it forms golden butter granules and the buttermilk separates from the butter – now stop whisking
  • pour cold water into a bowl. I added ice to tap water.
  • lift your butter out of the buttermilk and wash it in the cold water – press it, press it until all the milkiness is out.
  • the milk left behind in the bowl you took the butter from is buttermilk. Use it to bake bread with.
  • put your butter in a container in the fridge

Now you are expected to sit back comfortably and enjoy the butter on freshly baked bread or on hot toast. I eat nothing else with the butter, the butter is rich and delicious enough on its own.

If you want salted butter, you can work salt in once you've finished washing the butter. The amount of salt will depend on your palate.

Another tick next to an item on your bucket list.
